items.docs: Retrieve a list of all integration instances configured in your Zluri account.

params.name: instance_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the integration instance.

items.docs: Retrieve details of a specific integration instance.

params.name: instance_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the integration instance.

items.docs: Update an existing integration instance configuration with new data in the request body. You can update notification emails and other instance settings.

params.name: instance_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the integration instance.

items.docs: Create a new sync session for the specified instance. Only one sync can be active per instance at a time. Unfinished syncs block new syncs for the same instance. Data uploaded during a sync will not appear in Zluri until the sync is finished.

params.name: instance_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the integration instance.

params.name: sync_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the sync session.

items.docs: Retrieve the current status of a sync session. Use this to check if a sync is running, failed, or finished.

params.name: instance_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the integration instance.

params.name: sync_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the sync session.

items.docs: Mark a sync session as finished. Data will not appear in Zluri until the sync is finished. Always finish your sync after uploading all data. Once finished, a new sync can be created for the same instance.

params.name: instance_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the integration instance.

params.name: sync_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the sync session.

items.docs: Upload snapshot entity data within an active sync session. Snapshot data represents the current state of entities. Zluri compares new snapshots with the previous state to identify changes such as new users, deactivated users, and role changes. Data must be sent in paginated batches of up to 1000 records per page. Re-uploading a page with the same page number during an active sync will overwrite the previous data for that page. Supported entities include users, applications, contracts, and transa

params.name: instance_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the integration instance.

params.name: sync_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the sync session.

items.docs: Upload fact entity data within an active sync session. Fact data represents historical events that Zluri appends to the timeline for analytics and compliance tracking. Data must be sent in paginated batches of up to 1000 records per page. Fact and snapshot entities have different required fields and structures; using the wrong endpoint will fail schema validation.
Retrieve all configured webhooks for your Zluri account.
Create a new webhook to receive real-time notifications for events such as user status updates from IDP and HRMS tools.

params.name: webhook_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the webhook.

items.docs: Retrieve details of a specific webhook.

params.name: webhook_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the webhook.

params.name: webhook_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the webhook.
